title: no routing after reboot
body:
### Issue Description

I've set up two networks (one internal, one external) and some quadlets. For example a postgresql db (internal network) and a mail server (internal network to access db and external network for internet access). After a system reboot, routing is broken and the quadlets in the external network cannot reach the internet. There are error messages from aardvark-dns in the log `dns request failed: io error: Network is unreachable (os error 101)`.

I can stop all containers, do a `podman network reload --all` and start the containers again. This fixes the problem and routing works … until next reboot.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create quadlets and networks (in my case a internal and a external network)
2. Reboot the system
3. Try to ping an external host from a container which is part of the external network


### Describe the results you received

Routing does not work and internet hosts are not reachable.

`podman unshare --rootless-netns ip r` shows only

```
10.89.0.0/24 dev podman1 proto kernel scope link src 10.89.0.1 
10.89.1.0/24 dev podman2 proto kernel scope link src 10.89.1.1 
```

### Describe the results you expected

Routing works and intenet hosts are reachable.

`podman unshare --rootless-netns ip r` shows something like

```
default via 37.xxx.xxx.1 dev ens3 proto dhcp metric 1002 
10.89.0.0/24 dev podman1 proto kernel scope link src 10.89.0.1 
10.89.1.0/24 dev podman2 proto kernel scope link src 10.89.1.1 
37.xxx.xxx.0/22 dev ens3 proto dhcp scope link metric 1002 
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

vServer with Debian testing.

### Additional information

Issue happens every time after a system reboot.

Here are some of my quadlets/networks:

```
[Network]
NetworkName=netint
DisableDNS=false
Internal=true
```

netext.network:
```
[Network]
NetworkName=netext
DisableDNS=false
Internal=false
```

```
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

comments.body:
> Are you using NetworkManager? [#25656](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25656)

No, I use the default debian interfaces (ifupdown).

There are perhaps some relevant log messages during (re)boot:

```
pasta[1068]: Couldn't get any nameserver address
[...]
aardvark-dns[1227]: 17118 dns request failed: io error: Network is unreachable (os error 101)
```
Btw, in the meantime, I have switched from ifupdown to systemd-networkd. But it hasn't changed anything.

Do you have a working network-online.target setup?
I guess the NetworkManager doesn't matter here as this likely is some small race before the network is ready and pasta is started without proper routes https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25656#issuecomment-2748633736.

I would say we should continue the discussion there for now.
In any case it would be useful if someone could capture addresses and routes on the host when pasta is started and/or proves the logfile is described in the other issue.
